title: HTTPS and Secure Websites
drDigitalIntro: When a website is secure, its URL starts with https:// (the 's' stands for secure). A lock icon appears in the address bar. This means your connection is encrypted — information you send (passwords, credit card numbers, personal data) is scrambled and safe. If a site is just http:// without the 's', it's not secure. Never enter passwords or credit card numbers on an unsecured site. Check for the lock icon before entering sensitive information.
drDigitalSuccess: You know how to spot secure websites!
drDigitalHint: https:// + lock icon = safe to share passwords and payment info.
